note: Official values come from manufacturer specs or manuals. Horizontal and vertical FOV values for the Razer Kiyo Pro are inferred from the official diagonal FOV options assuming a 16:9 active image.

razer-kiyo-pro.label: Razer Kiyo Pro
razer-kiyo-pro.notes: Official spec exposes 80, 90, and 103 degree diagonal FOV modes. For a 4.6 m full-wall board, those modes cover that width at about 3.14 m, 2.64 m, and 2.11 m respectively. The virtual room starts farther back so all calibration tags are comfortably in frame.

logitech-c920.label: Logitech C920 / C920e family
logitech-c920.notes: Using Logitech's C920e optical spec as the closest official family reference. A 4.6 m full-wall board fills the horizontal frame at about 3.26 m camera-to-board distance. The virtual room starts farther back so the full board and inboard calibration tags are visible.

epson-co-fh02.label: Epson CO-FH02
epson-co-fh02.notes: Official Epson distance table gives 104-141 in lens-to-screen distance for a 100 in 16:9 image, which is about 1.19-1.61 throw ratio. Scaled to a 4.6 m full-wall board, the practical lens-to-board range is about 5.47-7.41 m.

aaxa-p6.label: AAXA P6 Ultimate
aaxa-p6.notes: P6 Ultimate is the HP-P6U-01 1100-lumen WXGA DLP smart projector, not the older 600-lumen P6. Throw ratio remains 1.2, putting a 4.6 m full-wall board image at roughly 5.52 m lens-to-board distance. Native resolution is 1280x800; 4K is accepted/scaled input, not native output.
